documentation:
[![CI](https://github.com/DiamondLightSource/bluesky-stomp/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/DiamondLightSource/bluesky-stomp/actions/workflows/ci.yml)
[![Coverage](https://codecov.io/gh/DiamondLightSource/bluesky-stomp/branch/main/graph/badge.svg)](https://codecov.io/gh/DiamondLightSource/bluesky-stomp)
[![PyPI](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/bluesky-stomp.svg)](https://pypi.org/project/bluesky-stomp)
[![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-Apache%202.0-blue.svg)](https://opensource.org/licenses/Apache-2.0)

# Bluesky Stomp

STOMP integration for bluesky

Source          | <https://github.com/DiamondLightSource/bluesky-stomp>
:---:           | :---:
PyPI            | `pip install bluesky-stomp`
Releases        | <https://github.com/DiamondLightSource/bluesky-stomp/releases>

## Low Level API

The library comes with some helpers for interacting with a stomp broker:

```python
from bluesky_stomp.messaging import MessageContext, StompClient
from bluesky_stomp.models import Broker, MessageQueue, MessageTopic

# Assumes you have an unauthenticated broker such as ActiveMQ running on localhost:61613
client = StompClient.for_broker(Broker(host="localhost", port=61613))

try:
    # Connect to the broker
    client.connect()

    # Send a message to a queue and a topic
    client.send(MessageQueue(name="my-queue"), {"foo": 1, "bar": 2})
    client.send(MessageTopic(name="my-topic"), {"foo": 1, "bar": 2})

    # Subscribe to messages on a topic, print all messages received,
    # assumes there is another service to post messages to the topic
    def on_message(message: str, context: MessageContext) -> None:
        print(message)

    client.subscribe(MessageTopic(name="my-other-topic"), on_message)

    # Send a message and wait for a reply, assumes there is another service
    # to post the reply
    reply_future = client.send_and_receive(
        MessageQueue(name="my-queue"), {"foo": 1, "bar": 2}
    )
    print(reply_future.result(timeout=5.0))
finally:
    # Disconnect at the end
    client.disconnect()
```

functions.name: bluesky_stomp.utils.handle_all_exceptions
documentation:
Ensure any uncaught exception traceback is printed to stdout. This does not
happen by default in threads other than the main thread. This function can
also be used as a decorator.

Args:
    func (Callable[..., Any]): The function to wrap
    callback (Optional[Callable[[Exception], None]], optional): Error handling
                                                                function, defaults
                                                                to printing a stack
                                                                trace to stderr.

Returns:
    Callable: Wrapped function that prints exception traceback

modules.name: bluesky_stomp._version
documentation: file generated by setuptools_scm
don't change, don't track in version control

classes.name: bluesky_stomp.models.BasicAuthentication
documentation: User credentials for basic authentication

classes.name: bluesky_stomp.models.Broker
documentation: Details required to connect to a message broker

classes.name: bluesky_stomp.models.DestinationBase
documentation: Base class for possible destinations of stomp messages

classes.name: bluesky_stomp.models.MessageQueue
documentation: Represents a queue (unicast) on a stomp broker

classes.name: bluesky_stomp.models.TemporaryMessageQueue
documentation:
Represents a temporary queue (unicast) on a stomp broker,
the broker may delete the queue after use

classes.name: bluesky_stomp.models.ReplyMessageQueue
documentation:
Represents a temporary queue (unicast) on a stomp broker specifically
for replying to other messages. Needed because RabbitMQ uses reply
queues and ActiveMQ uses temp queues.

classes.name: bluesky_stomp.models.MessageTopic
documentation: Represents a topic (multicast) on a stomp broker

functions.name: bluesky_stomp.serdes.serialize_message
documentation:
Pydantic-aware serialization routine that can also be
used on primitives. So serialize(4) is "4", but
serialize(<model>) is a JSON string.

Args:
    obj: The object to serialize

Returns:
    str: The serialized object

functions.name: bluesky_stomp.serdes.deserialize_message
documentation:
Deserialize a message into a useful type

Args:
    message: Message received from stomp
    obj_type: Target type for deserialization

Returns:
    T: An instance of obj_type

functions.name: bluesky_stomp.serdes.determine_callback_deserialization_type
documentation:
Inspect a function that is intended for listening
to messages and determine the type for deserialization based on the
type hints in the function signature.

Args:
    listener: A function that takes at least one parameter, the deserialized message
    default: A default type to fall back on if no type is supplied.
    Defaults to str.
    parameter_index: The index of the message parameter in the function signature.
    Defaults to 0.

Raises:
    IndexError: If parameter_index is out of bounds for the number of parameters the
    function actually takes

Returns:
    type[Any]: The type for deserialization

classes.name: bluesky_stomp.messaging.MessageContext
documentation: Context that comes with a message, provides useful information such as how to reply

classes.name: bluesky_stomp.messaging.StompReconnectPolicy
documentation: Details of how often stomp will try to reconnect if connection is unexpectedly lost

classes.name: bluesky_stomp.messaging._Subscription
documentation:
Details of a subscription, the client needs its own representation to
defer subscriptions until after connection

classes.name: bluesky_stomp.messaging.StompClient

methods.name: bluesky_stomp.messaging.StompClient.__init__
documentation:
Constructor

Args:
    conn: STOMP connection object
    reconnect_policy: Policy for how to handle unexpected disconnection.
    Defaults to the default settings of StompReconnectPolicy.
    authentication: Authentication credentials, if required. Defaults to None.
    serializer: Function to serialize messages to send to the broker.
    Defaults to serialize_message.
    deserializer: Function to deserialize messages from the broker.
    Defaults to deserialize_message.

methods.name: bluesky_stomp.messaging.StompClient.for_broker
documentation:
Alternative constructor that sets up a connection from host details

Args:
    broker: Details of the message broker

Returns:
    StompClient: A new client

methods.name: bluesky_stomp.messaging.StompClient.send_and_receive
documentation:
Send a message expecting a single reply.

Args:
    destination: Destination to send the message
    obj: Message to send, must be serializable
    reply_type: Expected type of reply, used in deserialization.
    Defaults to str.
    correlation_id: An id which correlates this request with requests it
    spawns or the request which spawned it etc.
Returns:
    Future[T]: Future representing the reply

methods.name: bluesky_stomp.messaging.StompClient.send
documentation:
Send a message to a given destination, handle a reply if required

Args:
    destination: Where to send the message
    obj: Message content
    on_reply: Callback for a reply, if supplied, the broker will be asked
    to create a temporary queue for replies. Defaults to None.
    on_reply_error: How to handle any errors in processing replies.
    Defaults to None.
    correlation_id: Correlation ID to be optionally included in message
    headers. Defaults to None.

methods.name: bluesky_stomp.messaging.StompClient.listener
bluesky_stomp.messaging.StompClient.subscribe
documentation:
Subscibe to messages on a particular queue or topic

Args:
    destination: Where the messages will come from
    callback: Function for processing the messages.
    The message type is derived from the function signature.
    on_error: How to handle errors processing the message. Defaults to None.

methods.name: bluesky_stomp.messaging.StompClient.connect
documentation: Connect to the broker, blocks until connection established

methods.name: bluesky_stomp.messaging.StompClient.disconnect
documentation: Disconnect from the broker

Utility class with helper methods for communication with a STOMP
broker such as ActiveMQ or RabbitMQ.
